title: Blender CLI Quality-of-Life Improvements
description: A collection of CLI usability improvements identified through repeated use of the export/promote/validate workflow. Covers new commands, output trimming, observability, automatic validation, and state persistence.
definition-of-done: All accepted improvements are implemented and documented in --help output.
testing-plan: Run each new/modified command and verify output matches expectations.
todos: TODO : New command: `errors` — tail addon.log filtered to ERROR/WARNING lines, with optional --group filter and --clear flag to truncate the log before a test run
TODO : New command: `export-and-promote` — chain export --split-frames → promote in one step, accepting --group and --dest
TODO : New command: `deps` — show the dependency tree of a node group (which sub-groups it references, recursively)
TODO : Add --brief flag to `validate` — single-line pass/fail with vertex count and sub-group count instead of full JSON
TODO : Add --brief flag to `promote` — show counts (files copied, imports rewritten) instead of full file lists
TODO : Strip unhelpful fields from `export` output (e.g. helpers_used only when --verbose)
TODO : Expose orphan server handlers as CLI commands: verify_topology, get_csv_data, get_point_data, check_corner
TODO : Add post-promote auto-validate: `promote --validate` runs validate on the promoted group after copying
TODO : Add .blender-cli-state.json for remembering last --group, --dest, --blend-file across invocations
TODO : Add --last flag to export/promote/validate that re-uses the previous invocation's arguments from state file
unknowns: Should --brief be the default with --verbose for full output, or the reverse?
Should state file be per-repo (.blender-cli-state.json in repo root) or per-user?
Which orphan server handlers are actually useful from CLI vs. internal-only?
